transcript: Hi Breakpoint. Uh, I'm Dean from Blueshift. Uh, quick show of hands, anyone here ever heard of us before? Whoa, wow, that's like almost everyone. That's crazy. For anyone who's not familiar with what we do here at Blueshift, we are a mission-driven community of Solana core developers, strengthening Solana through open source development, tooling, and free, world-class education. We've been building in the Solana ecosystem since 2022, and in January of this year, we decided to found this company with five key pillars in mind by which we could strengthen Solana. The first pillar is Education. We noticed with Solana's meteoric rise that it's been harder than ever to source quality developers, and with the existing developer resources outdated or hard to find, we decided to do something about it. That's why at Accelerate New York, we released V1 of the Blueshift Learn platform. And since then, we've actually grown to over 50k monthly active users with over 250 unique graduates minting 250 NFT on-chain verified credentials in our over 30 courses and challenges. And today, we're going to take this to the next level with Blueshift Learn V3. We have three new killer features to make your learning experience even better: Paths, Points, and Perks. Paths. We noticed that while everyone loves our content and so many people have found it to be the go-to place to learn Anchor, Pinocchio, or even Assembly if you want to go really deep, we realized the need to create a more curated experience. So today at learn.blueshift.gg, we're releasing V3 which contains Paths, giving you a curated journey from A to B so you can decide whether you want to be an Anchor master, if you want to be an Assembly ninja, we have a curated learning progression for you to make it easier for you to get to where you want to go and achieve your goals. Secondly, Points. Not everybody wants to record their achievements on-chain. So we wanted to lower the barrier to entry by having an easy way for people to track their progress off-chain whilst making it feel like a rewarding and gamified experience. And finally, Perks. We want Solana developers to feel like learning to build on Solana is a fun, rewarding, and lucrative experience. And so that's why we're finding ways and partners who we can partner with to give back to developers, and if you go there today, you actually have exclusive access to the Blueshift Devnet and Testnet faucets, and stay tuned because we're going to find new and exciting ways to reward developers. The second thing we do is Open Source. If you've actually ever used anything in Solana since 2022, you may have been using our software. We are contributors to Pinocchio, Agave, Solana SDK, Mollusk, Anchor, and most recently even the LLVM compiler project. We also do Tooling, and this year we dropped three killer pieces of tech, including Winterwallet, solving for the quantum zero-day threat on Solana with a quantum-secure vault. Doppler, the fastest oracle update in the ecosystem. Just 20 CUs for an update. That's over 50% faster than the next best competitor, and this is currently being used live in production in multiple high-performance DeFi applications, including prop AMMs. And finally, sBPF and sBPF Linker, making Solana BPF assembly a first-class citizen amongst all other programming languages and enabling you to build Solana programs with off-the-shelf Rust. No more having your admin disable your security settings at the HFT firm to download this bash script of 2 gigabytes of a forked toolchain. We can do it today with off-the-shelf Rust, as it should be. We also run a Validator. If you have Solana to stake and are looking for someone to support, we would love to have you at stake.blueshift.gg to continue to support free open source education and resources. And finally, our Dev Shop. We've been building in Solana since 2022, and in that time we've worked with and for some of the best names in the industry, and we would love for you to be one of them. That's why Virtuals, one of the largest apps on Base, chose Blueshift as their dev shop of choice for their SVM expansion. If you are a Web2 or Web3 enterprise looking to make the shift and build on Solana, contact us today at blueshift.gg/labs. We'd love to help you. I've been Dean. That's all the time I have. Make the shift, build on Solana.

enrichment.generated_query: Blueshift Learn V3 Blueshift official announcement blog technical documentation Solana Breakpoint 2025

sources.title: Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
sources.snippet:
Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

sources.title: Updates to Our Moderation Process
sources.snippet:
Updates to Our Moderation Process - Bluesky
[
Bluesky butterfly logo
Bluesky](https://bsky.social/about)
[Bluesky butterfly logo](https://bsky.social/about)
[Blog](https://bsky.social/about/blog)Updates to Our Moderation Process
# Updates to Our Moderation Process
November 19, 2025
byThe Bluesky Team
Our community has doubled in size in the last year. It has grown from a
collection of small communities to a global gathering place to talk about what’s
happening. People are coming to Bluesky because they want a place where they can
have conversations online again. Most platforms are now just media distribution
engines. We are bringing social back to social media.
On Bluesky, people are meeting and falling in love, being discovered as artists,
and having debates on niche topics in cozy corners. At the same time, some of us
have developed a habit of saying things behind screens that we'd never say in
person. To maintain a space for friendly conversation as well as fierce
disagreement, we need clear standards and expectations for how people treat each
other. In October, we[announced our commitment to building healthier social
media](https://bsky.social/about/blog/10-31-2025-building-healthier-social-media-update)and updated our Community Guidelines. Part of that work includes improving how
users can report issues, holding repeat violators accountable and providing
greater transparency.
Today, we're introducing updates to how we track Community Guidelines violations
and enforce our policies. We're not changing what's enforced - we've streamlined
our internal tooling to automatically track violations over time, enabling more
consistent, proportionate, and transparent enforcement.
## What's Improving
As Bluesky grows, so must the complexity of our reporting system. In Bluesky’s
early days, our reporting system was simple, because it served a smaller
community. Now that we’ve grown to 40 million users across the world, regulatory
requirements apply to us in multiple regions.
To meet those needs, we’re rolling out a significant update to Bluesky’s
reporting system. We’ve expanded post-reporting options from 6 to 39. This
update offers you more precise ways to flag issues, provides moderators better
tools to act on reports quickly and accurately, and strengthens our ability to
address our legal obligations around the world.
Our previous tools tracked violations individually across policies. We've
improved our internal tooling so that when we make enforcement decisions, those
violations are automatically tracked in one place and users receive clear
information about where they stand.
This system is designed to strengthen Bluesky’s transparency, proportionality,
and accountability. It applies our Community Guidelines, with better
infrastructure for accurate tracking and to communicate outcomes clearly.
## In-app Reporting Changes
Last month, we introduced updated[Community
Guidelines](https://bsky.social/about/support/community-guidelines)that provide
more detail on the minimum requirements for acceptable behavior on Bluesky.
These guidelines were shaped by community feedback along with regulatory
requirements. The next step is aligning the reporting system with that same
level of clarity.
Starting with the next app release, users will notice new reporting categories
in the app. The old list of broad options has been replaced with more specific,
structured choices.
**For example:**
* You can now report**Youth Harassment or Bullying**, or content such as**Eating Disorders**directly, to reflect the increased need to protect youth from harm on social media.
* You can flag**Human Trafficking content**, reflecting requirements under laws like the UK’s*Online Safety Act*.
This granularity helps our moderation systems and teams act faster and with
greater precision. It also allows for more accurate tracking of trends and harms
across the network.
![Illustration showing our new reporting categories. On the left are top level categories like 'Misle

tags.impact_reason: Blueshift's V3 launch with paths, points and perks provides solid updates to developer tooling and educational resources within the Solana ecosystem.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a holographic blueprint as the Semantic Anchor because it directly visualizes Blueshift's role as 'architects' of the Solana ecosystem, as mentioned in the standfirst. This concept embodies their work in building infrastructure, creating learning 'Paths', and migrating protocols. The blue glow is a direct nod to the company name, 'Blueshift'. For the homepage text, I integrated 'SVM' (Solana Virtual Machine) into the hologram. This is a core technology Blueshift develops for and teaches, representing the foundational layer upon which they build, making it a perfect diegetic detail.
visual_idea.description: In a cavernous, dark chamber with minimalist concrete architecture, a complex holographic blueprint of a futuristic digital structure glows intensely with a vibrant blue light. The projection emanates from a sleek, dark console, representing the intricate infrastructure Blueshift is building on Solana. Volumetric light rays from the hologram cut through the ambient haze. Diegetically integrated within the projection, the letters 'SVM' are visible as a foundational label on a central pillar of the digital architecture, as if etched in light. The background fades into deep shadow, suggesting immense scale and focus.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, cavernous, dark chamber crafted from sleek, polished concrete, featuring minimalist architecture that emphasizes its immense scale. At the heart of this vast space, a sleek, dark console projects a complex holographic blueprint of a futuristic digital structure. The blueprint glows intensely with a vibrant, ethereal blue light, vividly representing the intricate infrastructure Blueshift is building on Solana. Powerful volumetric light rays from the hologram slice through the ambient haze, illuminating dust particles and creating a dramatic atmosphere. Diegetically integrated within the glowing projection, the letters 'SVM' appear as a foundational label on a central pillar of the digital architecture, meticulously etched in light, as if part of the holographic construct itself. The upper background gradually fades into deep, profound shadow, enhancing the epic sense of height, negative space, and focused isolation. This composition is rendered in a 3:4 vertical aspect ratio, emphasizing the towering presence of the holographic structure. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, architectural model, holographic blueprint, polished concrete, volumetric lighting.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a holographic blueprint as the Semantic Anchor because it directly visualizes Blueshift's role as 'architects' of the Solana ecosystem, as mentioned in the standfirst. This concept embodies their work in building infrastructure, creating learning 'Paths', and migrating protocols. The blue glow is a direct nod to the company name, 'Blueshift'. For the homepage text, I integrated 'SVM' (Solana Virtual Machine) into the hologram. This is a core technology Blueshift develops for and teaches, representing the foundational layer upon which they build, making it a perfect diegetic detail.
visual_idea.description: A macro, top-down view focusing on a dense intersection within the blue holographic blueprint. Intricate, glowing lines of light representing data pathways and architectural connections converge and branch out. The image captures the immense complexity and precision of the system's design, with a focus on the geometric patterns and the subtle glow of particulate matter that forms the hologram. The underlying dark console is barely visible, providing a textured, out-of-focus base.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A stunning macro, top-down view capturing a dense intersection within a pristine blue holographic blueprint. Intricate, vibrant glowing lines of light, representing complex data pathways and architectural connections, converge and branch out with absolute precision. The image is a showcase of immense complexity and meticulous system design, highlighting exquisite geometric patterns and the ethereal, subtle glow of particulate matter that forms the dynamic hologram. The underlying dark console, with a cool, polished industrial texture reminiscent of polished concrete, is barely visible, providing a soft, out-of-focus base that grounds the luminous projection and reflects faint, cool light. Volumetric lighting permeates the scene, emphasizing depth and the radiant quality of the holographic elements. Composed for a 1:1 square aspect ratio, the image features strong symmetry and a central focus, with tight macro framing that fills the entire frame with intricate holographic textures and glowing details. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, cinematic lighting, ultra sharp focus.

editorial.headline: Virtuals Protocol Taps Blueshift for Solana Expansion; Quantum-Secure Wallet Unveiled
editorial.standfirst: The institutional developer shop cements its role in the SVM ecosystem by architecting the migration of Base’s leading AI protocol and releasing high-frequency trading infrastructure that undercuts competitors by 50%.
key_takeaways: **Virtuals Protocol** (a top app on Base) selected Blueshift Labs to architect its expansion onto the Solana Virtual Machine (SVM).
The new **Doppler** oracle executes updates in just 20 Compute Units (CUs), offering a 50% speed advantage over existing high-performance solutions.
**sBPF Linker** now enables developers to build Solana programs using standard, off-the-shelf Rust, removing the need for custom forks or toolchains.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The narrative that Solana is merely a "retail casino" is dismantling as infrastructure players like **Blueshift** roll out tooling that specifically targets institutional risk and high-frequency trading (HFT) efficiency. In a keynote that pivoted sharply from education to hard engineering, Blueshift lead Dean Little confirmed that **Virtuals Protocol**—a dominant application on the Base L2—has retained the firm to spearhead its migration to Solana. This signals a growing trend of "EVM-to-SVM" expansion where high-throughput applications are hitting the ceiling of optimistic rollups and seeking native execution speeds.

For the HFT and DeFi audience, the immediate alpha lies in **Doppler**, a new oracle protocol already live in production with proprietary market makers. By optimizing data feeds down to a mere 20 Compute Units (CUs), Doppler is purportedly 50% faster than its nearest competitor. In an ecosystem where block space is contested and latency defines profitability, an oracle that creates less compute overhead allows for tighter spreads and more complex on-chain strategies without hitting transaction limits.

Beyond speed, Blueshift addressed the "tail risk" that keeps compliance officers awake: quantum computing. The release of **Winterwallet** introduces a quantum-secure vault designed to neutralize zero-day threats from future computing breakthroughs. Coupled with the release of **sBPF Linker**—which allows engineers to compile Solana programs using standard, off-the-shelf Rust rather than custom forks—Blueshift is effectively removing the proprietary friction that has historically deterred large Web2 and institutional engineering teams from entering the ecosystem.
